project.name: CapSched-Linux
project.purpose: Introduce a capability-oriented execution and resource authority model into Linux scheduler foundations, eventually targeting monitor-backed domain isolation suitable for datacenter OS use.

linux_source.slice0a_status: applied and build-validated: upstream baseline, CONFIG_CAPSCHED=n, and CONFIG_CAPSCHED=y vmlinux builds completed successfully under systemd user runner
linux_source.slice0b_status: applied and build-validated: type-only authority scaffolding in include/linux/capsched.h and kernel/sched/capsched.c; CONFIG_CAPSCHED=n/y vmlinux builds completed successfully

active_analysis.current_focus: Linux Slice 0B is applied and build-validated as commit 7cf0b1e415bcead8a2079c8be94a9d41aad7d462. The assurance-case foundation exists in capsched-models/assurance/. After that gate, the safer next Linux-facing choice was source coverage rather than immediate trace patching: analysis/0019 maps try_to_wake_up, ttwu_runnable, remote wakelist, wake_up_new_task, move_queued_task, affinity changes, pick_next_task, __schedule, sched_class contracts, and sched_ext enqueue custody. Next: draft a strict Slice 0C trace-only implementation gate tied to EXEC-001, COMPAT-001, and G2, with no behavior changes.

architecture_intent.prototype_track: Linux-only CapSched L0 for performance, integration, and semantic exploration. It must not claim hypervisor-grade isolation.
architecture_intent.production_track: Monitor-backed CapSched-H: Linux-compatible domain-aware kernel plus small HyperTag Monitor enforcing non-forgeable DomainTag, epochs, MemoryView, IOMMU/queue ownership, and root CPU budgets.
architecture_intent.datacenter_goal: Provide process-to-container-granularity domains with isolation strength comparable to VM boundaries, while preserving lower cost and Linux ABI compatibility.
architecture_intent.cluster_goal: Support a cluster-wide capability/resource lease namespace without requiring one monolithic distributed kernel.

core_concepts.Domain: Schedulable protection, ownership, audit, and resource context. May represent a process, service, container, tenant, or cluster cell.
core_concepts.DomainTag: Active protection context selected at context switch; binds domain identity, epoch, capability root, MemoryView, object/device views, and side-channel policy.
core_concepts.RunCap: Authority to submit a specific thread/task for runnable execution. It must not include kill, suspend, priority, budget, affinity, spawn, or mint authority.
core_concepts.SchedContext: Independent CPU-time resource object containing budget, period, remaining runtime, allowed CPUs/NUMA, priority class, and co-tenancy constraints.
core_concepts.FrozenRunUse: Execution lease frozen at enqueue after RunCap and SchedContext checks. Runqueues must not store raw capability handles as authority.
core_concepts.EndpointCap: Resource-specific operation authority checked by resource endpoints such as VFS, socket, broker, device queue, or remote endpoint.
core_concepts.BudgetTicket: Bounded donated budget for broker/service execution, preventing service confused deputy and DoS amplification.
core_concepts.HyperTagMonitor: Small lower layer that enforces non-forgeable roots below Linux: domain registry, epochs, sealed run tokens, MemoryViews, IOMMU mappings, queue ownership, and root budgets.

accepted_invariants.text: No RunCap, no enqueue.
No SchedContext, no execution.
No budget, no execution.
No FrozenRunUse, no runqueue entry.
No valid epoch and generation, no execution.
No DomainTag activation, no context switch across domains.
No async work without caller provenance and frozen authority.
No caller budget, no broker/service execution on behalf of that caller.
Broker/service execution requires a live BudgetTicket reserved from caller budget.
Service authority alone must not execute caller work.
No FrozenEndpointUse, no async endpoint execution.
Linux credential override must not change CapSched DomainTag or frozen endpoint authority.
No raw pointer or mutable kernel object authority across Domain boundaries.
Linux-only prototypes must not claim hypervisor-grade isolation.
Mutable Linux DomainTag shadow state is not execution authority without monitor-owned activation.
No monitor RunToken, no active DomainTag or MemoryView for execution.
BPF and sched_ext may suggest or prototype policy, but must not be the production root for CapSched security invariants.
CapSched CPU placement must refine existing affinity, cpuset, sched-domain, housekeeping, and hotplug constraints rather than bypass them.
No cluster lease, mutable local shadow, or cached claim is directly executable; node-local execution and endpoint use require compiled LocalContext authority.
No live lease epoch, no node-local execution.
No monitor PageOwner, no Domain MemoryView mapping.
Linux page, memcg, slab, and page-cache metadata must not create cross-Domain memory authority.
Mutable page-cache data is not shared across Domains except through a typed service endpoint or sealed shared base.
Slab object lifetime or refcount validity is not capability authority without generation, epoch, and Domain checks.
Page revocation removes MemoryView mappings before the physical page is reused by another Domain.
Reclaim, writeback, and service memory work must carry caller provenance and a BudgetTicket when acting for a caller.
No Domain activation may carry untagged stale TLB translations into another Domain context.
No completed page revoke while MemoryView, direct-map, or TLB translations for that page remain.
Linux-visible direct-map shadow claims must not create hardware translation authority.
Mutable page-cache overlay state maps only into its owner Domain; sealed base content is the shareable form.
No stale overlay commit: page-cache overlay writeback requires provenance, ticket, current base version, and base-level serialization.
No QueueLease, no device queue doorbell or queue ownership transfer.
No monitor-owned IOMMU mapping, no DMA into a Domain MemoryView.
Interrupt delivery must be owned by the same Domain/service endpoint as the leased queue.
Linux shadow queue and IOMMU state must not create authority without a live monitor QueueTag, IOMMU map, and epoch.
Queue revocation invalidates submit, completion delivery, IOMMU map, in-flight DMA, and IRQ route together.
Queue rate or budget exhaustion prevents further doorbells even if Linux queue state says the queue is available.
No IRQ route aliasing across non-free queues.

design_boundaries: Do not decide exact Linux patch points before reading current upstream code.
Do not make RunCap a universal capability.
Do not place VFS, BPF, GPU, or protocol-specific semantics inside the scheduler.
Do not use sched_ext as the production security boundary; it is useful for policy exploration.
Do not claim hypervisor replacement without monitor-enforced memory, CPU, IOMMU, queue, epoch, and domain roots.
Do not treat validation as pass/fail plumbing; validation must support semantic security claims.
Do not treat TLC completion as the project objective; use TLC as one source of semantic evidence for the security and efficiency goals.
Every future Linux patch must name the assurance claim and gate it supports.

open_questions.text: Which upstream Linux version and exact commit should be used as the base?
open_questions.answer: Use git.kernel.org torvalds/linux at commit 4edcdefd4083ae04b1a5656f4be6cd83ae919ef4 on work branch capsched-linux-l0.

open_questions.text: What is the smallest L0 slice that can demonstrate RunCap/SchedContext/FrozenRunUse without destabilizing scheduler fast paths?
open_questions.answer: Slice 0A was accepted only as inert CONFIG_CAPSCHED scaffolding. No behavior-changing RunCap/SchedContext/FrozenRunUse slice is accepted yet.

open_questions.text: Which formal model should be written first: enqueue/pick/tick budget semantics, epoch revocation, or async provenance?
open_questions.answer: Start with the Runnable Lease Model: Task, TaskGeneration, ProcessGeneration, Domain, DomainEpoch, RunCap, SchedContext, FrozenRunUse, RunqueueState, CPU, and Budget. Keep async and monitor placeholders explicit.

open_questions.text: How should cluster-wide signed resource leases compile into local node SchedContexts and endpoint capabilities?
Should early io_uring endpoint provenance use direct CONFIG_CAPSCHED fields in io_kiocb/io_rsrc_node or side tables keyed by request/resource addresses?
Should registered endpoint revocation use eager cancellation or lazy execute-time rejection in Linux L0?
Should Linux L0 caller epoch revocation for broker tickets forfeit unspent reserved caller budget, refund it, or make that policy endpoint-specific?
open_questions.answer: The checked Broker BudgetTicket model uses strict caller-revoke forfeiture and service-revoke refund as a deliberately explicit baseline, not yet an accepted Linux policy.

open_questions.text: Should the first Linux Slice 0B introduce type-only endpoint/broker/domain authority scaffolding, or should cluster lease compilation be modeled first?
open_questions.answer: Model cluster lease compilation first, but do not gate progress on one giant full TLC run. The full ClusterLease systemd run was stopped after 17127406139 generated states, 550525279 distinct states, and no invariant error before interruption; decomposed ShadowForgery and EpochRevoke models then passed TLC.

open_questions.text: Should device isolation begin from VFIO/iommufd device assignment or from typed queue leases?
open_questions.answer: Begin from typed QueueLease semantics. Analysis 0016 says VFIO/iommufd is a compatibility substrate, while production authority is monitor-owned QueueTag, IOMMU map, IRQ route, epoch, and rate/budget. Formal 0011 QueueLease passed two TLC runs for the generic boundary; device-specific endpoint models remain future gates.

open_questions.text: Should TLC completion be treated as a project goal or as auxiliary validation evidence?
open_questions.answer: TLC is auxiliary evidence. The project goal is secure, efficient CapSched Domain separation. Large TLC runs should be split by semantic proof obligation rather than allowed to dominate design progress.

open_questions.text: Can Linux page metadata, memcg, or slab/page-cache state serve as the production Domain memory authority root?
open_questions.answer: No. Analysis 0017 says these are valuable accounting, lifetime, reclaim, and compatibility substrates, but production memory authority requires monitor-owned PageOwner and MemoryView mappings.

open_questions.text: Which remaining L2 memory risk should be modeled after MemoryOwnership?
open_questions.answer: Direct-map visibility and TLB revocation were modeled first. The first DirectMapTLB run found a stale TLB-on-Domain-switch counterexample; after requiring Domain activation to flush or retag translations, TLC completed with no invariant errors. Page-cache overlay conflict semantics remain a separate L2 gate.

open_questions.text: What page-cache overlay rule prevents stale Domain dirty state from overwriting a newer sealed base?
open_questions.answer: PageCacheOverlay found that per-overlay commit is insufficient. Commit/writeback requires provenance, ticket, current base version, and base-level serialization or an equivalent commit token.

next_actions.text: Initialize Git in capsched/ for project-control artifacts, with linux/ left unignored as a sibling Linux repository.
Fetch current upstream Linux into sibling directory linux/ and record exact commit metadata.
Read scheduler, fork/exec/exit, cgroup CPU, core scheduling, sched_ext, workqueue, and io_uring code before choosing patch points.
Create investigation notes that map actual upstream code paths to CapSched invariants.
Select and scope the first formal semantic model before implementing prototype code.
Write and check the Runnable Lease formal model before selecting Linux L0 patch points.
Model EndpointCap and async provenance for socket and io_uring after the Runnable Lease model.
next_actions.partial_progress: Endpoint Async Provenance TLA+ model was written and checked. TLC explored 291297 generated states and 37392 distinct states with no invariant errors. Validation record: capsched/capsched-models/validation/0005-endpoint-async-tlc.md.

next_actions.text: Derive the Linux L0 implementation plan from the checked Runnable Lease model and source-analysis maps before touching Linux code.
Review the candidate Linux L0 Runnable Lease implementation plan and explicitly choose the first no-behavior-change Linux patch slice.
Run the baseline out-of-tree Linux build and then apply/validate Slice 0A inert CONFIG_CAPSCHED scaffolding.
next_actions.partial_progress: Slice 0A Linux commit 0b685979f27b3d42ee620ced5f707ee391a2a27f is applied and validated. systemd user runner completed upstream baseline, CONFIG_CAPSCHED=n, and CONFIG_CAPSCHED=y vmlinux builds. CONFIG_CAPSCHED=n produced no capsched object; CONFIG_CAPSCHED=y produced kernel/sched/capsched.o. Log: /media/nia/scsiusb/dev/linux-cap/build/logs/l0-slice0-build-20260626T011458Z.log.

next_actions.text: Map the checked Endpoint Async Provenance model back to concrete Linux io_uring, workqueue, task_work, socket, and LSM attachment points.
next_actions.partial_progress: Recorded analysis/0015 and implementation/0003. Key finding: io_kiocb and io_rsrc_node are natural io_uring carriers; generic workqueue and task_work need wrappers; socket endpoint checks cannot rely only on LSM hooks because sendmmsg can reuse sock_sendmsg_nosec.

next_actions.text: Choose the next gate: Slice 0B type-only endpoint authority scaffolding, or a deeper Broker/BudgetTicket formal model before more Linux code.
next_actions.partial_progress: Chose the deeper Broker/BudgetTicket formal model before more Linux code. TLC explored 129777 generated states and 25008 distinct states with no invariant errors. Validation record: capsched/capsched-models/validation/0006-broker-budget-ticket-tlc.md.

next_actions.text: Choose the next gate after Broker/BudgetTicket validation: Slice 0B type-only endpoint/broker authority scaffolding, or DomainTag/monitor activation modeling.
next_actions.partial_progress: Chose DomainTag/monitor activation modeling before more Linux behavior changes. TLC primary and second runs each explored 82993249 generated states and 1916784 distinct states with no invariant errors. Validation record: capsched/capsched-models/validation/0007-domain-monitor-activation-tlc.md.

next_actions.text: Choose the next gate after Domain Monitor Activation validation: Slice 0B type-only endpoint/broker/domain authority scaffolding, or a cluster lease compilation model.
next_actions.partial_progress: Chose Cluster Lease Compilation modeling. Full ClusterLease integration model was too large for chat supervision and is being moved to a systemd user service without weakening the model.

next_actions.text: Monitor the systemd user ClusterLease full integration TLC run and update validation record.
next_actions.partial_progress: Run was stopped by user request after 17127406139 generated states, 550525279 distinct states, and 512945750 states left on queue. No invariant error was observed before interruption, but this is not a pass. Validation record 0008 was updated and the proof root moved to decomposed models.

next_actions.text: Split cluster authority validation into smaller proof obligations without weakening the security model.
next_actions.partial_progress: Added Formal 0007. ClusterShadowForgery passed TLC with 20646297 generated states and 1513736 distinct states. ClusterEpochRevoke passed TLC with 231929 generated states and 23688 distinct states. Validation record: capsched/capsched-models/validation/0009-cluster-authority-decomposition-tlc.md.

next_actions.text: Review the Slice 0B readiness gate with the decomposed cluster authority validation before applying any Linux type-only authority scaffolding patch.
next_actions.partial_progress: Updated implementation/0004 so Slice 0B is no longer blocked on full ClusterLease TLC completion, but remains limited to inert type scaffolding, opaque cluster names, no hot struct fields, no behavior, no user ABI, and no Linux-only security claims.

next_actions.text: Before any L4 device work, model QueueLease semantics for queue ownership, IOMMU mappings, interrupt routes, epochs, and rate budgets.
next_actions.partial_progress: Added formal/0011 QueueLease. After strengthening IRQ aliasing, TLC completed two finite runs with different fingerprint indexes; each explored 97882849 generated states and 6465312 distinct states with no invariant errors. Validation record: capsched/capsched-models/validation/0013-queue-lease-tlc.md.

next_actions.text: Before any L2 per-Domain MM work, model MemoryOwnership semantics for page ownership, MemoryView mapping, slab object generation, page-cache overlays, and reclaim provenance.
next_actions.partial_progress: Added formal/0008. The broad MemoryOwnership integration model was stopped after growth without invariant errors, then split into PageOwnerMemoryView, SlabObjGen, and MemoryWorkProvenance. All three decomposed models passed TLC. Validation record: capsched/capsched-models/validation/0010-memory-ownership-tlc.md.

next_actions.text: If the user accepts Slice 0B, apply only inert type scaffolding in include/linux/capsched.h and kernel/sched/capsched.c, then repeat CONFIG_CAPSCHED=n/y build validation.
Map upstream MM allocator, slab, memcg, and page-cache structures to CapSched Domain memory authority boundaries.
next_actions.partial_progress: Added analysis/0017. Key result: Linux page, memcg, slab, and page-cache metadata are useful substrate but not non-forgeable Domain memory authority; MemoryOwnership should be modeled before L2 MM work.

next_actions.text: Before any real L2 MM implementation, choose and model the next memory risk: direct-map visibility, TLB shootdown ordering, or page-cache overlay conflict semantics.
next_actions.partial_progress: Chose direct-map visibility and TLB shootdown ordering. Added formal/0009 DirectMapTLB. The first run found a stale TLB entry surviving Domain activation; the model was fixed by requiring activation to flush or retag translations. Final TLC explored 8224001 generated states and 386784 distinct states with no invariant errors. Validation record: capsched/capsched-models/validation/0011-direct-map-tlb-tlc.md.

next_actions.text: Before any real page-cache overlay implementation, model overlay conflict semantics for sealed shared bases, per-Domain mutable overlays, writeback, and service mediation.
next_actions.partial_progress: Added formal/0010 PageCacheOverlay. The first run found two overlays could commit concurrently against the same sealed base version; one could advance the base while the other remained stale committing. The model was fixed by requiring base-level commit serialization. Final TLC explored 7370677 generated states and 524808 distinct states with no invariant errors. Validation record: capsched/capsched-models/validation/0012-page-cache-overlay-tlc.md.

next_actions.text: Choose the next gate after L2 memory-risk modeling: Slice 0B inert type-only scaffolding or QueueLease modeling before L4 device work.
next_actions.partial_progress: Chose QueueLease modeling before returning to Linux code. Formal 0011 passed two TLC runs and validates the generic queue/IOMMU/IRQ/budget lease boundary.

next_actions.text: Choose the next gate after generic QueueLease validation: Slice 0B inert type-only scaffolding, or a device-specific QueueLease endpoint model for NIC, NVMe, GPU, or VFIO compatibility.
next_actions.partial_progress: Chose Slice 0B inert type-only scaffolding in parallel with an assurance-case track. Device-specific QueueLease endpoint models remain future L4 gates, not the immediate next step.

next_actions.text: Apply Slice 0B inert type-only scaffolding only in include/linux/capsched.h and kernel/sched/capsched.c, then repeat CONFIG_CAPSCHED=n/y build validation.
next_actions.partial_progress: Linux commit 7cf0b1e415bcead8a2079c8be94a9d41aad7d462 adds type-only authority scaffolding only in include/linux/capsched.h and kernel/sched/capsched.c. Validation 0014 passed: CONFIG_CAPSCHED=n/y vmlinux builds completed; disabled config produced no kernel/sched/capsched.o and enabled config produced kernel/sched/capsched.o.

next_actions.text: Create an assurance-case subclaim tree that maps top-level hypervisor-replacement claims to models, Linux evidence, monitor evidence, gaps, and forbidden claims.

TOP-001 is decomposed into activation, execution, budget, endpoint, async, memory, TLB, page-cache, device, revoke, cluster, compatibility, TCB, side-channel, and evaluation subclaims with evidence, gaps, counterexamples, forbidden L0 claims, and gate criteria.

next_actions.text: Select the next Linux-facing gate: Slice 0C trace-only Domain observation or a narrower wakeup/enqueue/runnable-state source coverage analysis.
next_actions.partial_progress: Selected the narrower wakeup/enqueue/runnable-state source coverage analysis before any trace patch. Added analysis/0019, which shows activate_task alone is insufficient and maps self wake, already-runnable wake, delayed fair requeue, remote wakelist, new task wake, queued migration, affinity changes, pick/core scheduling, switch, sched_class contracts, and sched_ext enqueue custody.

next_actions.text: Draft a strict Slice 0C trace-only implementation gate using analysis/0019 and assurance claims EXEC-001, COMPAT-001, and G2.
